title: ruscus hypophyllum
intro: Ruscus hypophyllum, also known as the Polynesian button fern or button fern, is a type of plant native to the Pacific Islands, specifically in New Caledonia and Fiji. The medicinal properties of Ruscus hypophyllum have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been traditionally used to provide relief from coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems, as well as to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Ruscus hypophyllum can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its numerous health benefits.
